data.title: Nexans S.A. reports FY results
data.description:
• Nexans S.A. (OTCPK:NXPRF): FY Net income at €283 million, up +27% versus 2023
  • Revenue of €7.1B (+8.7% Y/Y)

data.title: Daily Journal reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Daily Journal press release (NASDAQ:DJCO): Q1 effective tax rate for the three months ended December 31, 2024 was 26.9%, after including the taxes on the unrealized gains on marketable securities.
  • Revenue of $17.7M (+10.7% Y/Y).

data.title: New Zealand gets another 50-bp rate cut, more easing expected this year
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of New Zealand has cut its benchmark interest rate by 50 basis points to 3.75%, in an effort to boost economic activity while inflation pressures continue to ease. This is its third 50-bp cut, with rates being lowered 175 bps since August, when it started its easing cycle.

New Zealand's economy slipped into recession in the third quarter of 2024. The RBNZ noted that economic activity remains subdued, but lower rates and higher export earnings are expected to support a modest recovery this year.

"Lower interest rates will encourage spending, although elevated global economic uncertainty is expected to weigh on business investment decisions," it said, pointing to U.S. tariff policies.

The central bank sees more rate cuts this year "if economic conditions continue to evolve as projected." RBNZ Governor Adrian Orr projected two 25-bps cuts in April and May, with the rate reaching around 3% by year-end.

Consumer price inflation remains near the midpoint of its 1%-3% target band. "The economic outlook remains consistent with inflation remaining in the band over the medium term," the RBNZ said.

The kiwi dollar (NZD:USD) weakened against the greenback soon after the monetary policy move, but has since stabilized at around $0.572.

The RBNZ's decision comes a day after Australia's central bank reduced its cash rate for the first time since late 2020. "Early aggressive easing by the RBNZ suggests New Zealand can be better prepared to face trade headwinds than Australia," said Francesco Pesole, FX strategist, ING.

data.title: Straumann reports FY results
data.description:
• Straumann Holding AG press release (OTCPK:SAUHF): FY GAAP EPS of CHF 3.14.
  • Revenue of CHF 2.5B (+13.7%)
  • Fourth-quarter revenue showed 11.5% organic growth, amounting to CHF 645 million
  • Core EBIT margin at 26% including currency impact, or 27.6% at constant 2023 currencies, and core net profit at CHF 502 million
  • Implant system iEXCEL launched in key markets, digital platform AXS globally available
  • High employee engagement score of 82, with a 92% survey response rate
  • Board proposes further dividend increase to CHF 0.95
  • Outlook 2025: organic revenue growth in the high single-digit percentage range, with a 30 to 60 basis points improvement of the core EBIT margin at constant 2024 currency rates

data.title: Caledonia Mining names new finance chief
data.description:
Caledonia Mining (NYSE:CMCL) said that its CFO, Chester Goodburn, will step down from his role on 24 March 2025, following FY 2024 earnings release.

He will be succeeded, with immediate effect, by Ross Jerrard, subject to satisfaction of standard checks.

Mr Goodburn has served as Caledonia’s CFO since July 1, 2022, before which he had been Caledonia’s Group Financial Manager and Chief Information Officer.

Mr Jerrard, aged 50, brings extensive experience in financial leadership, having been the CFO of Centamin plc from April 2016 until its acquisition by AngloGold Ashanti.

data.title: Citigroup veteran Russell Chong to leave after ~27 years - report
HSBC Holdings declares $1.80/ADS dividend; initiates share buy-back of up to $2B
data.description:
• HSBC Holdings (NYSE:HSBC) declares $1.80/ADS interim dividend.
  • The dividend will be payable on ADSs, each of which represents five ordinary shares, on 25 April 2025 to holders of record on 7 March 2025; ex-div March 7.
  • The board approved a fourth quarterly dividend of $0.36 per share, bringing the total dividend announced for 2024 to $0.87 per share. This includes the special dividend of $0.21 per share that was paid in June following the completion of the sale of HSBC Bank Canada.
  • "In addition, the bank announced three share buy-backs in respect of 2024 worth a total of $9B. And today, we announced a further share buy-back of up to $2B."
  • See HSBC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Philips reports Q4 results, gives 2025 outlook
data.description:
• Philips press release (NYSE:PHG): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.51.
  • Revenue of €5.04B (-0.4% Y/Y).
  • For 2025, Philips expects:
  • 1%-3% comparable sales growth, including a mid- to high-single-digit decline in China
  • Adjusted EBITA margin increasing 30-80 bps to 11.8%-12.3%
  • Free cash flow before payment of the USD 1.1 billion cash-out relating to the US medical monitoring and personal injury settlements will be at the lower end of the range of EUR 1.4 billion to EUR 1.6 billion. Net of this cash-out, free cash flow will be EUR 0.4 billion to EUR 0.6 billion.
  • Sales of EUR 18.0 billion in 2024, comparable sales growth 1%; EUR 5.0 billion in Q4, comparable sales growth 1%, despite double-digit decline in China
  • Comparable order intake increased 1% in 2024; up 2% in Q4, despite double-digit decline in China • Income from operations was EUR 529 million in 2024; EUR 199 million in Q4
  • Adjusted EBITA margin increased 90 basis points to 11.5% of sales in 2024; up 60 basis points to 13.5% in Q4
  • Net cash flow from operating activities was EUR 1,569 million in 2024; EUR 1,459 million in Q4
  • Free cash flow was EUR 906 million in 2024; EUR 1,285 million in Q4
  • Increased productivity savings target for 2023-2025 from EUR 2 billion to EUR 2.5 billion, EUR 800 million in 2025

data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals eyes higher production in 2025, projects 40% growth in next five years
data.description:
Wheaton Precious Metals (NYSE:WPM) Tuesday reported 2024 actual production of over 633,000 gold equivalent ounces ("GEOs), exceeding the upper end of the company’s guidance range, and said that it expects higher output in the current year.

The company sees 2025 production in the range of 600,000 to 670,000 GEOs and growth of approximately 40% to 870,000 GEOs by 2029.

This forecast growth is driven by stronger attributable production from Antamina, the start-up of several development projects, and a stable forecast for Salobo production, which is expected to be largely offset by lower production from Peñasquito and Constancia, the Canadian precious metals company said.

In 2024, gold equivalent production exceeded the upper limits of the guidance range, primarily resulting from stronger-than-expected production at Salobo due to higher gold grades and recoveries, and higher grades at Constancia from the mining of the Pampacancha deposit.

These outperformances were partially offset by lower-than-expected production from San Dimas and Zinkgruvan, in both instances due to lower grades, the company said.

As at December 31, 2024, approximately 163,850 GEOs were produced but not yet delivered representing approximately three months of payable production.

Wheaton (NYSE:WPM) will provide full production and financial details with the release of its 2024 fourth quarter and full year results on Thursday, March 13, after market close.

data.title: VAALCO Energy declares $0.0625 dividend
data.description:
• VAALCO Energy (NYSE:EGY) declares $0.0625/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 5.73%
  • Payable March 28; to shareholders of record Feb. 28; ex-div Feb. 28.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.0625 for nine consecutive quarters.
  • See EGY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
